title: Trump Renews Threat to Fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook After Supreme Court Ruling
summary: President Trump promised to “take appropriate action immediately” against Lisa Cook, the Fed governor he had tried to fire, even as the court affirmed that Fed officials can be fired only for cause.

title: Supreme Court Expands Trump’s Power to Fire Officials but Prevents Lisa Cook Removal
summary: In twin rulings, the Supreme Court affirmed the Fed’s independence and said its leaders could not be fired at will, but said President Trump could fire other independent regulators for any reason.

title: Supreme Court Decision on Late-Arriving Mail-In Ballots Grants Relief to 18 States
summary: The Supreme Court ruled on Monday that state laws allowing ballots to arrive after Election Day are legal. The decision is the latest in a series of setbacks for President Trump’s efforts to regulate elections.

title: Supreme Court Rejects Trump’s Request to Appeal $5 Million Verdict in E. Jean Carroll Case
summary: President Trump had asked the justices to intervene after a jury found that he had sexually abused and defamed the writer E. Jean Carroll.

title: ‘No One’s Coming to Save Us but Us’: Gen Z Runs for Office
summary: Melat Kiros’s challenge Tuesday to Representative Diana DeGette, who was elected the year before Ms. Kiros was born, is the latest Gen Z test in a year defined by generational upheaval.

title: California Hopes to Speed Up Election Results With $40 Million Infusion
summary: It took six days to determine the outcome of the primary for Los Angeles mayor, and seven for the governor’s race.

title: Kean Set to Speak at the Capitol After Mysterious Absence
summary: After months of silence, Representative Tom Kean Jr. was expected to deliver a speech explaining his 116-day absence from Congress.

title: Two Dan Sullivans to Appear on Alaska Senate Ballot, State High Court Rules
summary: Republicans had sought the removal of a little-known candidate with the same name as the incumbent senator, arguing that he was not a “good faith” candidate.

title: Judge Orders Trump to End Efforts to Kill Hudson Tunnel Funding
summary: A federal judge says that the administration’s attempts to block money for the $16 billion rail project were “flagrantly” illegal.

title: Trump on Legislation to Address the Nation’s Housing Crisis: ‘It’s a Yawn’
summary: Time and again, President Trump has brushed off Americans’ concerns about the economy and their financial situations.

title: Clash Unfolds as Trump Administration Pushes Intelligence Agencies to Share Foreign Espionage Targets
summary: The clash reflects increasing skepticism of the Office of the Director of National Intelligence, which has been willing to pursue President Trump’s priorities.

title: Moscow Is Attacked by Dozens of Drones, Its Mayor Says
summary: Sergei Sobyanin said the Russian military had shot down more than 60 drones. He did not report any injuries.

title: In San Francisco’s A.I. Era, Even $180,000 Tech Salaries Are No Longer Enough
summary: As OpenAI and Anthropic prepare to go public, tech workers making six figures are grousing that they cannot compete with the new A.I. elite. Some doubt they can afford to stay.

title: A.I. ‘Employees’ Might Disrupt Work in Unexpected Ways
summary: Scholars say the “unknown unknowns” of using artificial intelligence in the workplace may be undermining the technology’s advertised benefits.

title: $22,000 Per Hour: Assistants Use a Legislative Loophole to Outearn Surgeons
summary: A law meant to end surprise medical billing has led to large paydays for some surgical assistants, who can earn far more than the doctors they help.

title: Former Chinese Billionaire and Bannon Associate Is Sentenced for Fraud
summary: The businessman courted U.S. conservatives as an anti-Communist crusader while using his supporters’ money to buy lavish homes and a Bugatti supercar.

title: See Day-by-Day Forecast as Heat Wave Engulfs U.S. Ahead of the July 4 Weekend
summary: Dangerous heat is expected across much of the country through the Fourth of July weekend.

title: Emily Barker Loved Fighting Fires. She Died in One She Couldn’t Escape.
summary: Emily Barker was one of two women who died fighting a large forest fire in Colorado this week.

title: What to Know About Wildfires in Colorado and Other Western States
summary: Thousands of acres in Utah and other Western states have burned. Here is what to know about the firefighters who have died, the destruction so far and the spreading smoke.

title: How the Wildfires in Colorado and Utah Were Poised to Spark
summary: Since last week, meteorologists issued a series of increasingly dire warnings about extremely critical weather conditions.

title: In Peru’s Presidential Election, Keiko Fujimori Gives the Right in Latin America Another Win
summary: Keiko Fujimori, a daughter of the former strongman Alberto Fujimori, returns her family’s movement to power, but with a narrow victory in a divided country.

title: Pope Leo’s Showdown With the Church’s Radical Right
summary: An impending clash between Pope Leo and ultratraditionalist Catholics threatens to end the pontiff’s honeymoon.

title: Venezuela’s Tragedy Is Bigger Than the Earthquakes
summary: Venezuela’s natural disaster was unavoidable, but the devastation it has left in its wake was not.

title: John Oliver Dives Into Trump’s Pet Project: A ‘Giant Duck Death Trap’
summary: The “Last Week Tonight” host said the United States was “dealing with a literal swamp of corruption” for its 250th birthday.

title: 6 Are Killed During a Rare Mass Shooting in Germany
summary: The authorities said a 45-year-old man opened fire at a youth facility after a quarrel about the custody of his baby daughter. The dead were all facility employees.
